chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P. No. D-6604 of 2020 __________________________________________________________________ Order with signature of Judge 1. For order on CMA No.10438/2021. (Urgency) 2. For order on office objection. 3. For order on CMA No.28262/2020. 4. For hearing of CMA No.28263/2020. 5. For hearing of main case. 12.04.2021 Mr. Ghulam Husain, advocate holding brief for Mr. Rafiq Ahmed Kalwar, advocate for the petitioner along with petitioner. Mr. Ali Safdar Depar, AAG. ------ 1. Urgency granted. Petitioner, who is present in person, states that he is an educationist and has been serving in the Public Education Sector in the Province of Sindh since 1987. Petitioner invited the attention of this Court of the order dated 22.12.2020 passed by this Court and submitted that urgency is required in this matter because Chairman of two Boards i.e Matric and Intermediate have already been appointed on an ad-hoc basis despite the availability of efficient officials. He further pointed out that this Court vide order dated 16.03.2021 directed the learned AAG to submit the report of the Intelligence Agency, if any, with an affidavit but nothing has been done. At this juncture, learned AAG, who is present in the Court in other matters, intervened and submitted a copy of the summary for the Chief Minister Sindh dated 09.03.2020 along with a bunch of documents for the appointment of Chairman in various Boards of Sindh. He also relied upon the Special Report of the petitioner and submitted that petitioner has been blamed for certain financial irregularities in his previous employment in different tenders including deducting staff salaries; and, he does not carry a good reputation, as per the report of Intelligence Bureau, as such he was not considered for appointment for the subject post.

At this juncture, learned AAG, who is present in the Court in other matters, intervened and submitted a copy of the summary for the Chief Minister Sindh dated 09.03.2020 along with a bunch of documents for the appointment of Chairman in various Boards of Sindh. He also relied upon the Special Report of the petitioner and submitted that petitioner has been blamed for certain financial irregularities in his previous employment in different tenders including deducting staff salaries; and, he does not carry a good reputation, as per the report of Intelligence Bureau, as such he was not considered for appointment for the subject post. The aforesaid allegations were denied by the petitioner, however, he requests for supply of the copy. Copy of the aforesaid documents is directed to be provided to the petitioner during the course of the day. Prima-facie, this matter requires an urgent hearing on the aforesaid points. Let it be fixed for tomorrow i.e. 13.04.2021 at 11.00 a.m when the Secretary University and Board Department Government of Sindh should be in attendance along with an affidavit in support of the allegations leveled against the petitioner, in terms of order dated 16.03.2021. JUDGE JUDGE Nadir*
